restore.notes: Replace module name, version, and connection metadata with values discovered from the active workspace. Reconcile any app-specific expect or restore structures against a real current blueprint before activation.

metadata.notes: Template only. Replace placeholders with discovered app and connection data before deployment. This file is a provisioning starter, not proof that the scenario is activatable. After create or update, explicitly patch the scenario-level interface before the first run. For the generic shell contract, keep ReturnData mapped to {{3.body}} so ExpectDataAny receives the raw response body; validate runs to confirm body contains the intended payload or error object. If a provider module mishandles empty bodies on GET or DELETE, keep this shell contract but consider a read shell without the Module 2 body mapper and a separate write shell with it.
